assumptions: The question is assessed in the consolidated financial statements of the group.
The intragroup dividend has already created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable.
The receivable/payable is denominated in a way that gives rise to foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation, as contemplated by IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable exposed to foreign currency risk can be considered under the fair value hedge model if the IFRS 9 intragroup FX exception is met.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a cash flow hedge model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss, so it is a distinct hedge accounting treatment to consider.
